revision_note: v3 integrates 3-way review findings: codex source-read corrections (kimi_cli/* at ~/.local/share/uv/tools/kimi-cli/lib/python3.13/site-packages/) and probe 06 fresh-path upsert confirmation.

stream_json.format_actual: JSONL — one JSON line per printed message. A single kimi run may emit MULTIPLE lines when tool use occurs (each tool_result is a separate role='tool' event; each assistant turn is a separate role='assistant' event). Not per-token streaming; granularity is per printed message.
stream_json.content_block_types_unknown_handling: Phase 1 should skip unknown types when concatenating assistant text; preserve raw block for debugging but do not error.
stream_json.tool_calls_location: Assistant event may carry a tool_calls field outside content[] (source: kosong/message.py); Phase 1 does not need to render these for /kimi:ask but must not drop them silently for /kimi:rescue.
stream_json.stats_exists_internally_but_dropped_by_j: kimi_cli/wire/types.py defines StatusUpdate(token_usage, context_tokens) but kimi_cli/ui/print/visualize.py JsonPrinter drops it via case _: pass. No flag combination surfaces this in --output-format stream-json.
stream_json.stderr_session_emission_path: kimi_cli/cli/__init__.py::_print_resume_hint via _emit_fatal_error writes directly to sys.stderr; NOT gated by --quiet flag. Only at-risk if the *caller* discards stderr (e.g. 2>/dev/null) — emission itself is reliable.

work_dirs.new_entry_evidence: Probe 06: /tmp/kimi-fresh-<pid> had no prior entry; after kimi call, a new work_dirs entry with path and last_session_id was appended. Source: kimi_cli/session.py::Session.create() calls new_work_dir_meta() + save_metadata() when get_work_dir_meta() misses.
work_dirs.path_storage_details: Source code calls canonical() which normalizes (absolute + dot-segment removal) but does NOT resolve symlinks. Probe 06 confirmed: input /tmp/kimi-fresh-32940 was stored verbatim; md5 of this string matches the session dir. md5 of realpath /private/tmp/... does NOT match. Phase 1 implication: always pass -w with the same form used for comparison (recommend fs.realpathSync(cwd) on both sides).

large_prompts.rationale: stdin avoids ARG_MAX across platforms; macOS handles up to 200k inline but Linux often has lower limits.

auth_ping.caveat: Ping with invalid model will create a wasted session (LLMNotSet path). Phase 1 should pre-validate model name against ~/.kimi/config.toml models list before calling --print.

sigterm_mid_stream.stdout_state: empty or partial, depending on whether LLM had emitted anything
sigterm_mid_stream.buffering_truth: kimi itself uses flush=True on every print (source: kimi_cli/ui/print/visualize.py). Empty stdout on SIGTERM means the LLM had not yet produced any content, NOT that kimi was buffering. SIGKILL terminates identically (no SIGTERM handler installed per kimi_cli/utils/signals.py).
sigterm_mid_stream.recovery: Treat as signal-terminated; no partial parse. Retry from scratch is safe.

invalid_model.internal_path: kimi_cli/app.py::create_llm() returns None when model name not found in config; kimi_cli/llm.py raises LLMNotSet; ui/print writes 'LLM not set' to stdout; session metadata is already saved (wasted session).
invalid_model.recovery: Phase 1 must pre-validate model against ~/.kimi/config.toml's [models.*] table before invocation. Surface clear error 'Model <X> not configured'. Do NOT use the wasted session id.

bad_cwd.stderr_format: text (Click CLI error box with Unicode borders)
bad_cwd.recovery: Surface fs error; ask user to create/correct path.

command_ux_exit_code_map.1: config error (likely 'LLM not set') — check stdout first line; surface as model-config error
command_ux_exit_code_map.2: CLI usage error (bad -w, bad flag) — show stderr Click error box content

implications_for_phase_1: callKimi parses stderr for session id — stdout JSON does not carry it; stderr emission is unconditional (not gated by --quiet), so only at risk if caller discards stderr.
Stream parser must handle MULTIPLE JSONL lines per run; do not assume single-line completion (tool_result and multi-turn cases produce multiple lines).
Assistant text extraction: iterate event.content[] where event.role=='assistant', filter block.type=='text', concat block.text. Skip unknown block types without error.
Think-type blocks: default policy is DROP (do not surface to user). Exposing them is a UX choice for kimi-result-handling skill to decide; spec §3.3 content_aggregation section codifies this.
No per-token streaming UX possible. Use a spinner during wait; render each completed message block as it arrives (paragraph-level increments for multi-line runs).
Large prompts (>100KB): pipe via stdin with -p "".

Secondary session-id fallback via kimi.json.work_dirs[cwd].last_session_id is viable; use when stderr is unavailable. Path comparison must use the SAME form on both sides (recommend realpath(cwd) consistently).
Fresh-path invocation is safe: kimi upserts work_dirs entries automatically on first call; no pre-flight mkdir needed.
Auth check uses --max-steps-per-turn 1 and pre-validates model name to avoid wasted sessions.
v0.1 cannot expose token/context stats — JsonPrinter drops StatusUpdate events. render.mjs stats path must be null-safe or omitted.
SIGKILL terminates kimi identically to SIGTERM (no graceful handler). Use SIGTERM for cancellations.
